intro.speaker: npc
intro.text: Hi! Sorry, I was just finishing an email to the account managers about... well, another change they didn't ask for.

intro_2.speaker: npc
intro_2.text: I'm Morgan. Five years of trying to help people through transitions they didn't want. You're the new AI lead, right?

assess.speaker: npc
assess.text: People don't resist change. They resist being changed. Remember that and we'll get along fine.

choices.text: What's the mood like around here?
Sage mentioned stakeholder alignment is critical.
I'm sure people will come around.

mood.speaker: npc
mood.text: Honestly? Tired. Falcon burned through a lot of goodwill. SmartRoute burned through the rest. People hear 'AI initiative' and they hear 'more disruption for no benefit.'

mood_2.speaker: npc
mood_2.text: The account managers especially. They've been promised AI tools three times now. Three times they reorganized their workflows. Three times the projects died.

history.speaker: npc
history.text: Project Falcon? Management sent a company-wide email on a Monday morning: 'AI is coming, embrace the future.' No context. No training. No warning to managers.

history_2.speaker: npc
history_2.text: Half the customer service team thought they were being replaced. Two people quit that week. We weren't even replacing anyone. The comms just... didn't explain that.

sage_connection.speaker: npc
sage_connection.text: Sage gets it. Strategy without buy-in is just a PowerPoint nobody reads. I can help make the strategy real — if people actually trust the process this time.

naive.speaker: npc
naive.text: That's what the last three project leads said. And the one before that. People don't 'come around.' They get brought along, or they dig in.

closing.speaker: npc
closing.text: I want this to work. I really do. But it has to be done right this time. Talk to me before you announce anything to the team.

check_in.speaker: npc
check_in.text: Hey. How's the change management side looking? Please tell me we have a communication plan.

choices.text: I want your help building a change communication plan.
The stakeholder roadshow is going well.

comm_plan.speaker: npc
comm_plan.text: Yes! Finally! Okay. First, we need manager briefings before any team-wide announcements. Managers need to feel prepared, not ambushed.

comm_plan_2.speaker: npc
comm_plan_2.text: Second, weekly 'ask anything' sessions. Short. Honest. People need a place to voice concerns without feeling judged. I can facilitate those.

roadshow_feedback.speaker: npc
roadshow_feedback.text: I've been hearing good things! For the first time in years, people are cautiously optimistic. That's huge. Cautious optimism is Morgan-speak for 'not openly hostile.'

sentiment.speaker: npc
sentiment.text: Mixed. The early adopters are excited. The skeptics are watching. The middle — which is most people — are waiting to see if this one sticks.

sentiment_2.speaker: npc
sentiment_2.text: Win over the middle, and you win the organization. Ignore them, and you'll have a great tool that nobody uses.

terminal_angry.speaker: npc
terminal_angry.text: You sent a company-wide email. Without talking to me. Without a comms plan. Do you know how I found out? My inbox. Like everyone else.

terminal_angry_2.speaker: npc
terminal_angry_2.text: This is exactly what happened with Falcon. Management sends a blast email, nobody's prepared, managers get blindsided with questions they can't answer.

choices.text: You're right. I should have come to you first.
The message needed to go out. We can fix it.

terminal_forgive.speaker: npc
terminal_forgive.text: Thank you. Look — the damage is done on that one. But going forward, let me help you draft these. I can make sure the messaging lands right.

terminal_grudge.speaker: npc
terminal_grudge.text: Fix it. Sure. I'll just explain to 200 anxious employees that the email they're panicking about was sent without any comms strategy. That'll be a fun afternoon.
